summary: Merged stage-focused decomp PR split from #2345. The PR body/title list five `gr` functions as 100% matches: `grKinokoRoute_8020836C`, `fn_801F9038`, `grHomeRun_8021EC58`, `grPushOn_802190D0`, and `grOldKongo_80210650`. The work paired stage behavior matches with callback typedef/prototype cleanup and some broader formatting/include churn. Review concentrated on avoiding an ugly `__FILE__` override in `groldkongo.c` and clarifying why the LObj type check in `grpushon.c` was inlined instead of redefining/calling the existing baselib helper.

key_files.reason: Reworked `grKinokoRoute_8020836C` with `Ground_GObj*`/`Item_GObj*` typing, JObj flag toggles, collision joint add/remove calls, and item material updates for item kind `0xA0`.
Implemented `fn_801F9038` and retuned nearby Ice Mountain callback/prototype code to return segment ids as `int`.
Matched `grHomeRun_8021EC58` text overlay creation using scale-derived sizes, white `GXColor`, and `HSD_SisLib_803A6B98(..., "%d", ...)`.
Defined Push On light descriptors/config table and matched `grPushOn_802190D0` light object setup loop, including an inlined LObj type check with explanatory comment.
Rewrote `grOldKongo_80210650` as a weighted random angle picker using stage data counters and named `grOk_804DBAxx` sdata2 float constants.
Updated Ice Mountain declarations for `fn_801F9038`, `fn_801F91A8`, and `grIceMt_801F9ACC`.
Added `GmRouteCallback` typedef used by game-mode route callback storage/calls.
Replaced raw `bool (*)(s32)` callback signatures with `GmRouteCallback` and removed casts at call sites.
Largest touched file in the slice, apparently stage cleanup/formatting/include-order work; PR body does not identify a new Venom match.

what_changed: The PR body says this was split from #2345 to contain only stage-related 100% matches.

`grKinokoRoute_8020836C` now uses a `Ground_GObj*` signature and iterates `HSD_GObj_Entities->items` as `Item_GObj*`, applying `grMaterial_801C8E08`/`grMaterial_801C8E28` while adding/removing collision joints `0x3C`, `0x33`, and `0x0C`-`0x0F`.

`fn_801F9038` was implemented for Ice Mountain: it calls `HSD_Randi(6)` until a segment slot has zero cooldown and does not equal the recent `xC4`/`xC6` ids, decrements other cooldowns, stores the cooldown from `grIm_804D69F4`, and returns `grIm_803E4068[idx].id`.

`grHomeRun_8021EC58` was changed to construct the Home-Run score text object with explicit white color, scale-derived box/font dimensions, left-aligned/fitting text settings, and the `"%d"` format at y `-29.0F`.
`grPushOn_802190D0` was added along with two `HSD_LightDesc` definitions and a 9-entry `grPushOn_LightConfig` table; the loop sets point-light flags, color, scaled position, and distance attenuation.

`grOldKongo_80210650` became an else-if weighted subtraction chain over fields `x2C` through `x3A`, returning named float constants such as `grOk_804DBA04` through `grOk_804DBA1C` and asserting with `HSD_ASSERT(786, 0)` if no bucket matches.
Callback typing was improved: `GmRouteCallback` and `GrIceMtSegmentLookup` replaced raw or casted function pointer types in `gm_801674C4`, `fn_80169434`, `fn_801F91A8`, and `grIceMt_801F9ACC`.

The PR body notes dropped work from #2345: `lbAudioAx_800243F4`, `fn_800244F4`, and `grOldPupupu_8021119C` were already upstream, while `grKinokoRoute_80207B5C` was left/reverted to a stub because it was only 99.64% with real struct-field offset diffs.

Evidence caveat: the included decomp-dev bot report appears to be for an intermediate comparison, showing only `fn_801F9038` as a new 100% match and `grPushOn_802190D0` at 93.16%, while the final PR title/body claim five 100% matches.
smart_moves: Kept the PR scoped to stage-related 100% matches instead of carrying the full #2345 backlog.
Did not force a 99.64% `grKinokoRoute_80207B5C` into a 100% PR when the remaining differences were real struct-field offset issues.
Introduced named callback typedefs to remove unsafe-looking casts and document actual callback signatures.

For `grPushOn_802190D0`, final code inlines the LObj flag mask and comments that it is equivalent to `HSD_LObjGetType` but required for original assembly, directly addressing review concern about shadowing the baselib function.
Used typed GObj aliases (`Ground_GObj`, `Item_GObj`) where possible, reducing repeated casts in stage code.
Preserved Old Kongo float/data matching with named extern sdata2 constants rather than scattering angle literals through the function.
Simplified the Old Kongo weighted random code into an else-if decrement chain; a reviewer noted a for-loop might be possible, but accepted the matching shape if loop codegen was not worth forcing.
decomp_lessons: For PRs advertised as 100% match progress, leave near-matches as stubs when remaining diffs are structural rather than cosmetic.
Callback typedefs are a reusable cleanup tactic when decompiled code has repeated casts like `HSD_GObjEvent` or `bool (*)(s32)` that obscure the actual return type.

When a real helper exists but calling it changes codegen, inline the exact flag operation and explain why; do not create a local macro/function with the same name as the real helper without a comment.
Named sdata2 float constants can be necessary for data/code matching, but reviewer feedback suggests avoiding `#undef __FILE__`/custom `__FILE__` tricks unless they are truly required and documented.
Exact GObj subtypes and item/stage-specific pointer types can make matching code cleaner while also documenting intent.
Formatting-only churn across unrelated files should be treated as support noise in future archaeology; search the PR body and removed `/// #` stubs to identify the real match payload.
naming_conventions: Stage functions keep the `grStageName_address` convention, e.g. `grKinokoRoute_8020836C`, `grHomeRun_8021EC58`, `grPushOn_802190D0`, and `grOldKongo_80210650`.
Unidentified functions remain `fn_address`, as with `fn_801F9038` and `fn_801F91A8`.
Stage data symbols use short stage prefixes plus addresses, e.g. `grIm_804D69F4`, `grOk_804DBA04`, `grPushOn_803E7B74`.
New helper types use descriptive subsystem names: `GmRouteCallback`, `GrIceMtSegmentLookup`, and `struct grPushOn_LightConfig`.
Reviewer suggested naming unused padding arrays as `x1C_pad...` rather than plain `x1C` when fields are known padding.
Headers retain address comments in prototypes, e.g. `/* 1F9038 */ int fn_801F9038(Ground_GObj*)`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(8)` in `fn_801F9038` and `PAD_STACK(16)` in `grKinokoRoute_8020836C` to preserve stack layout.
Kept Ice Mountain's segment selection as a `do { idx = HSD_Randi(6); } while (...)` rejection loop, matching cooldown and recent-segment checks.
Changed `fn_801F91A8` from pointer-like `HSD_GObj*` returns to `int` returns, eliminating pointer casts that could affect codegen and prototype correctness.
For Old Kongo, used `(r -= field) < 0` else-if comparisons to match weighted-bucket control flow.
For Old Kongo, referenced `grOk_804DBA04` through `grOk_804DBA1C` extern float constants instead of using inline angle literals.
For Push On, inlined `(u32) (lobj->flags & LOBJ_TYPE_MASK) == LOBJ_POINT` in the assert instead of calling/redefining `HSD_LObjGetType`.
For Home-Run text, preserved expression/cast details such as `grHr_804D6AE4 * Ground_801C0498() * 8.0F` and `(s32) (f32) arg`.

review_feedback.feedback: Questioned the early `extern char grOk_803E6640[]` plus `#undef __FILE__`/`#define __FILE__ grOk_803E6640` hunk: "This looks bad. Is this used? if not, probably remove".
review_feedback.outcome_observed: Final diff keeps the `grOk_804DBAxx` float externs but does not include that `__FILE__` override.

review_feedback.feedback: Noted the weighted-random chain might be expressible as a for-loop, but matching code was fine if a loop was not worth pursuing.
review_feedback.outcome_observed: Final diff uses a compact else-if subtraction chain.

review_feedback.feedback: Questioned a local `HSD_LObjGetType` definition because a real function exists in `lobj.c`; asked for clarification if it was for inline/matching reasons.
review_feedback.outcome_observed: Final diff removes the macro and adds a comment explaining the inlined LObj flag check is equivalent to the real helper but needed to match original assembly.

review_feedback.feedback: Suggested unused struct bytes be renamed to `x1C_pad...`; explicitly called it good practice and not a hard requirement.
review_feedback.outcome_observed: Slice does not provide a clear final diff for that exact padding hunk.

review_feedback.feedback: Review summary said only the `__FILE__` and LObj questions were hard requirements; the other two comments were suggestions.
review_feedback.outcome_observed: Review state in the slice is `DISMISSED`; PR later merged.

follow_up_queries: Find the final decomp.dev report for PR #2404 or its merge commit to reconcile the intermediate bot report with the body's five 100% match claim.
Search later PRs for `grKinokoRoute_80207B5C` to see whether the 99.64% struct-field offset issue was solved.
Audit other `gr` route callbacks still cast to `HSD_GObjEvent` or raw `bool (*)(s32)` for possible `GmRouteCallback`/stage-specific typedef cleanup.
Search `lobj.c` and later stage PRs for `HSD_LObjGetType` inline comments to reuse the Push On matching pattern.
Check subsequent `groldkongo.c` history for padding names like `x1C_pad` and for any replacement of the weighted-random chain with a matching loop.
